1: qah-lĕkā gillāyôn gādôl = "take a large tablet/scroll (gillāyôn, H1549)" — a polished writing board; large for public visibility. wĕktoḇ ʿālāyw bĕḥereṭ ʾĕnôš = "write on it with an ordinary stylus (ḥereṭ ʾĕnôš)" — in common/plain handwriting that anyone can read. The public inscription māhēr šālāl ḥāš baz = "swift is plunder, speedy is prey" — the prophetic summary of the Syro-Ephraimitic campaign's outcome.
2: wĕʾāʿîdâ lî ʿēdîm neʾĕmānîm = "I will take for myself faithful witnesses (ʿēdîm neʾĕmānîm)" — ʿēd (H5707) = legal witness; the inscription is formally testified before fulfillment. ʾet-Ûriyyâ hakkohen wĕʾet-Zĕkaryāhû ben-Yĕḇerekĕyāhû = Uriah the priest (probably the high priest who collaborated with Ahaz, 2 Kgs 16:10-16) and Zechariah son of Jeberechiah — credible court witnesses who validate the prophecy.
3: wāʾeqraḇ ʾel-hannĕḇîʾâ wattahar watteledabbēn = "I approached the prophetess and she conceived and bore a son" — the prophetess (nĕḇîʾâ) = Isaiah's wife (titled by her husband's prophetic role). The birth fulfills the Immanuel sign's near dimension: a young woman of Isaiah's household bears a son as a sign-child. wayyōʾmer YHWH qĕrāʾ šĕmô māhēr šālāl ḥāš baz = the name is YHWH's command — the child is a living sign-oracle.
4: kî bĕṭerem yēdaʿ hannaʿar qĕrōʾ ʾāḇî wĕʾimmî = "for before the child knows to call 'my father' and 'my mother'" — the linguistic-developmental marker: pre-speech, the earliest possible span. yiśśāʾ ʾet-ḥêl Dammāśeq wĕʾēt šĕlal Šōmrôn lipnê melek ʾAššûr = "the wealth of Damascus and the spoil of Samaria will be carried away before the king of Assyria." Near-fulfillment: Damascus fell 732 BCE, Samaria 722 BCE — within infancy of Maher-shalal-hash-baz. The child's infancy is the countdown clock.
5: wayyôsep YHWH dabbēr ʾēlay ʿôd = "and YHWH continued speaking to me again" — a new oracle appended, expanding judgment beyond the two northern kingdoms to include Judah itself, since Ahaz chose Assyria over YHWH.
6: yaʿan kî māʾas hāʿām hazzeh ʾet mê haššilōaḥ hahōlĕḵîm lĕʾaṭ = "because this people has rejected the waters of Shiloah that flow gently (lĕʾaṭ)." Shiloah (šilōaḥ) = the slow channel supplying Jerusalem's water from the Gihon spring (cf. John 9:7 Siloam). Its gently flowing water = YHWH's quiet, sufficient provision. Rejected in favor of the roaring Assyrian flood. wĕśimḥâ ʾet-Reṣîn ûben-Rĕmalyāhû = "and rejoices over Rezin and the son of Remaliah" — Judah's sympathy with the coalition rather than YHWH's plan.
7: hinnēh ʾădōnāy maʿăleh ʿălêhem ʾet-mê hannāhār hāʿăṣûmîm wĕhārabbîm = "the Lord is bringing up the waters of the River (hannāhār = the Euphrates), mighty and many." The contrast: mê haššilōaḥ hahōlĕkîm lĕʾaṭ (Shiloah's gentle stream) vs. mê hannāhār hāʿăṣûmîm (the Euphrates flood). Reject the quiet water; receive the flood. wĕʿālâ ʿal-kol-ʾăpîqāyw wĕhālak ʿal-kol-gĕḏōtāyw = "rising over all its channels and going over all its banks."
8: wĕḥālap bîhûḏâ šāṭap wĕʿāḇar ʿad-ṣawwāʾr yagîaʿ = "it sweeps into Judah, overflows and passes through, reaching even to the neck" — the flood reaches Judah's neck (just short of drowning, cf. 30:28). Not completely submerged: judgment but not annihilation. wĕhāyâ muttôt kĕnāpāyw mĕlōʾ rōḥaḇ ʾarṣĕkā ʿimmānûʾēl = "and the outspread of his wings will fill the breadth of your land, O Immanuel." Even in the flood-oracle, the Immanuel name resounds: the land belongs to Immanuel, not to Ahaz.
9: rōʿû ʿammîm wĕḥōttû = "Be broken (rāʿaʿ) O peoples, and be shattered (ḥātat)!" — the taunt to the nations: YHWH mocks their pretension. ʾizzĕrû wĕḥōttû ʾizzĕrû wĕḥōttû = "gird yourselves and be shattered; gird yourselves and be shattered" — the anaphora of futile military preparation (girding for battle only to be shattered).
10: ûṣû ʿēṣâ wĕtûpar daḇĕrû dāḇār wĕlōʾ yāqûm kî ʿimmānû ʾēl = "Take counsel and it will be frustrated; speak a word and it will not stand, for God is with us (ʿimmānû ʾēl)." The nations' plans are futile because of Immanuel. The name becomes a war-cry against the coalition. Note: ʿimmānû ʾēl here is YHWH's presence with Judah; in Matt 1:23 the same declaration is the name of the incarnate Son.
11: kî kōh ʾāmar YHWH ʾēlay kĕḥizqat hayyād wĕyisĕrēnî = "For thus YHWH said to me, with strong hand (kĕḥizqat hayyād)" — the grip of prophetic compulsion; the same phrase used of Ezekiel's prophetic experiences (Ezek 3:14; 8:1). lĕbiltî lēket bĕdereḵ hāʿām hazzeh = "not to walk in the way of this people" — Isaiah warned not to adopt the political fearfulness of the people. The prophet must operate from a different epistemology.
12: lōʾ-tōʾmrûn qešer lĕkōl ʾăšer-yōʾmar hāʿām hazzeh qāšer = "Do not call conspiracy (qeśer) everything that this people calls a conspiracy" — the people's fear-driven conspiracism. wĕʾet-môrāʾô lōʾ-tîrāʾû wĕlōʾ taʿărîṣû = "do not fear what they fear, do not be in dread (ʿāraṣ)" — two different words for dread and terror. The prophet must not be shaped by collective political anxiety.
13: ʾet-YHWH ṣĕḇāʾôt ʾōtô taqddîšû = "YHWH of armies — him you shall sanctify (qiddaš, piel)." wĕhûʾ môrāʾăkem wĕhûʾ maʿărîṣăkem = "he shall be your fear and he shall be your dread" — the same two words for terror (v12) are now redirected: YHWH becomes the object of the awe that displaces political fear. 1 Pet 3:14-15 cites this verse: "Do not fear their threats... but sanctify Christ as Lord."
14:
wĕhāyâ lĕmiqdāš ûlĕʾeben negeṗ ûlĕṣûr mikšôl = "he will become a sanctuary (miqdāš) and a stone of stumbling (ʾeben negeṗ) and a rock of offense (ṣûr mikšôl)" — the same divine presence is both sanctuary (refuge) and stumbling stone (occasion of fall). ûlĕpāḥ ûlĕmôqēš lĕyôšēḇ Yĕrûšālaim = "and a trap and a snare for the inhabitants of Jerusalem."

The stone imagery is absorbed into NT Christology: Rom 9:33 ("a stone of stumbling and a rock of offense"); 1 Pet 2:8 ("a stone of stumbling and a rock of offense"); Jesus cites Ps 118:22 (the rejected stone becoming cornerstone, Luke 20:17-18) in the same framework. The stumbling stone that is also a sanctuary is the tension resolved only in Christ: those who trust him find refuge; those who reject him stumble on him.
15: wĕkāšĕlû bām rabbîm wĕnāpĕlû wĕniśbārû wĕnôqĕšû wĕnilkādû = "and many shall stumble (kāšal) on them and fall and be broken and be snared and be taken" — five verbs cascading: fall → breaking → ensnaring → capture. Total disaster for those who treat YHWH's word as an obstacle. Paul applies this to Israel's stumbling over the gospel (Rom 9:32-33); Peter applies it to those who reject the cornerstone (1 Pet 2:8).
16: ṣôr tĕʿûḏâ ḥătôm tôrâ bĕlimmûdāy = "Bind up the testimony (tĕʿûḏâ), seal the teaching (tôrâ) among my disciples (limmûdāy)." Limmûdāy = those who have been taught/disciples. This is the earliest use of a "disciples" concept in the prophets: Isaiah has a circle of learners who will preserve the prophetic word when the general population rejects it. The sealing is for safekeeping until fulfillment demonstrates its truth.
17: wĕḥikkîtî laYHWH hammastîr pānāyw mibbayt yaʿăqōb = "and I will wait for YHWH who is hiding his face (hammastîr pānāyw) from the house of Jacob." Ḥāḵâ (H2442) = to wait with earnest expectation; wĕqiwwîtî lô = "and I will hope in him" — qāwâ (H6960) = the wait of expectation (same word as 40:31). Heb 2:13 directly cites this verse ("I will put my trust in him") as words of Christ — the Servant/Son trusting the Father in the hiddenness of incarnation/passion.
18: hinnēh ʾānōkî wĕhayĕlādîm ʾăšer nātan-lî YHWH lĕʾōtôt ûlĕmôpĕtîm bĕyiśrāʾēl = "Behold, I and the children whom YHWH has given me are signs (ʾōtôt) and portents (mōpĕtîm) in Israel." Isaiah and his sign-children (Shear-jashub = "a remnant shall return"; Maher-shalal-hash-baz = "swift is plunder") are living oracles. mēʿim YHWH ṣĕḇāʾôt haššōkēn bĕhar-ṣiyyôn = "from YHWH of armies, who dwells on Mount Zion." Heb 2:13 directly quotes this verse, placing it in Christ's mouth: "Behold, I and the children God has given me" — the children = those who share Christ's flesh and blood, those he calls brothers (Heb 2:11-14).
19: wĕkî yōʾmĕrû ʾălêkem dirĕšû ʾel-hāʾōḇôt wĕʾel-hayyiḏdĕʿōnîm = "when they say: Consult the mediums (ʾōḇôt) and the spiritists (yiḏdĕʿōnîm)." ʾĀḇôt = "ghost-pits" or the spirits summoned through them; yiḏdĕʿōnîm = "knowing ones," familiar spirits. hameṣapṣĕpîm wĕhammĕhagîm = "who chirp and mutter" — the sound-description of spirit-communication (cf. Matt 12:43-45 where Jesus uses spirit-language in a related context). hălôʾ ʿam ʾel-ʾĕlōhāyw yiḏrōš = "should not a people consult their God?" — the obvious counter: YHWH is the living God who speaks through prophets.
20: lĕtôrâ wĕlitĕʿûḏâ = "To the tôrâ and to the testimony!" — the standard is YHWH's revealed word, the tôrâ-tĕʿûḏâ pair = the whole prophetic deposit (cf. v16). ʾim-lōʾ yōʾmĕrû kaḏḏāḇār hazzeh ʾăšer ʾên-lô šāḥar = "if they do not speak according to this word, there is no dawn (šāḥar) for them." The test: those who speak against tôrâ have no light. The darkness they inhabit is their judgment — until the dawn of ch9 breaks.
21: wĕʿāḇar bāh niqšeh wĕrāʿēḇ wĕhāyâ kî-yirʿaḇ wĕhitqaṣṣap = "they will pass through the land, hard pressed and hungry; when they are hungry they will be enraged (qāṣap)." The social unraveling of judgment: hunger and desperation. wĕqillēl bĕmalkô ûḇĕʾĕlōhāyw ûpānâ lĕmāʿĕlâ = "and will curse their king and their God, looking upward" — blame God and king in anger rather than turning to God in prayer.
22: wĕʾel-ʾereṣ yabiṭ wĕhinnēh ṣārâ waḥăšēḵâ = "and they will look to the earth, and behold, distress (ṣārâ) and darkness (ḥăšēḵâ)." Looking up: cursing; looking down: anguish. No direction offers relief. ûʾăpēlâ mĕnuddāḥ = "and into thick darkness (ʾăpēlâ) they are driven away (nāḏaḥ)" — ends at the darkest point. But this is the pivot: ch9:1-7 opens immediately with the people who walked in darkness have seen a great light — the Immanuel oracle finds its completion in the Light that breaks from this very darkness.
